evals.prompt:
Please audit this login form for accessibility issues: ```html
Email:
Password:
Login
```
evals.expected_output: A structured accessibility audit report with severity-rated findings covering: missing form labels, non-semantic button element, missing alt text on image, and potentially insufficient color contrast. Each finding should include WCAG criterion, severity, current/fixed code snippets.
expectations: Report includes a summary with total finding count and severity breakdown
At least one finding addresses missing or inadequate form labels (WCAG 3.3.2 or 1.3.1)
At least one finding addresses the non-semantic button div (WCAG 4.1.2 or 2.1.1)
At least one finding addresses the image missing alt text (WCAG 1.1.1)
Each finding includes a corrected code snippet
Each finding references a specific WCAG criterion

evals.prompt:
Audit this navigation component for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance: ```html
Home About Contact ☰
```
evals.expected_output: An audit report identifying: low contrast on links (#aaa on white fails 4.5:1), contact link removed from keyboard focus (tabindex=-1), and menu icon lacking accessible name and keyboard access.
expectations: Report flags low color contrast issue (WCAG 1.4.3) for #aaa colored links
Report flags the tabindex=-1 on Contact link as a keyboard accessibility issue
Report flags the onclick span as missing keyboard support and accessible name
Severity levels are assigned to each finding
Fixes are provided for at least the contrast and keyboard issues

evals.prompt:
I need an accessibility audit of our modal dialog component. It opens when a button is clicked, shows content, and has a close button. But users have complained it traps keyboard focus and screen readers announce the wrong thing. Here's the pattern we're using: ```html

Confirm Action

Are you sure you want to delete this item?

```
evals.expected_output: A detailed audit covering modal-specific a11y patterns: missing role=dialog and aria-modal, no focus trap implementation, vague close button label, missing aria-labelledby, and no focus return on close.
expectations: Report identifies missing role=dialog or dialog element
Report identifies keyboard focus trap requirement (WCAG 2.1.2)
Report identifies the 'X' close button as having an insufficient accessible name
Report recommends aria-labelledby or aria-label for the modal
Report addresses focus management (return focus to trigger on close)
